payload.query: What explains the relationship between executives and legislatures? What is the most important factor(s) that determine this relationship?

articles.title: Read the Supreme Court’s Decision on Trump’s Sweeping Tariffs
articles.abstract: In a major setback for President Trump’s economic agenda, the court ruled that he could not invoke an emergency power to set tariffs on imports.
articles.snippet: In a major setback for President Trump’s economic agenda, the court ruled that he could not invoke an emergency power to set tariffs on imports.

articles.title: Read the Supreme Court Decision in the Birthright Citizenship Case
articles.abstract: In the citizenship case, the justices voted 6-3, with the liberals in dissent, to limit the lower-courts’ preliminary injunctions to each plaintiff with standing to sue. The majority stressed that it is not addressing the merits of Trump’s attempt to end automatic citizenship for babies born on U.S. soil to undocumented migrants and foreign visitors without green cards.
articles.snippet: In the citizenship case, the justices voted 6-3, with the liberals in dissent, to limit the lower-courts’ preliminary injunctions to each plaintiff with standing to sue. The majority stressed that it is not addressing the merits of Trump’s attempt to end automatic citizenship for babies born on U.S. soil to undocumented migrants and foreign visitors without green cards.

articles.title: Read the Court Ruling on Trump’s Use of the Alien Enemies Act
articles.abstract: The ruling was a setback for the Trump administration on the mass deportation of immigrants, one of its domestic policy goals.
articles.snippet: The ruling was a setback for the Trump administration on the mass deportation of immigrants, one of its domestic policy goals.

articles.title: Read the appeals court ruling on Trump’s tariffs.
articles.abstract: An appeals court ruled on Friday that many of President Trump’s most punishing tariffs are illegal, delivering a major new setback to the administration’s global trade war.
articles.snippet: An appeals court ruled on Friday that many of President Trump’s most punishing tariffs are illegal, delivering a major new setback to the administration’s global trade war.

articles.title: Is This the Most Important Supreme Court Case of the Century?
articles.abstract: The tariff ruling may have just helped save the Republic.
articles.snippet: The tariff ruling may have just helped save the Republic.

articles.title: Nine Law Firms Surrendered. Four Law Firms Won.
articles.abstract: The four law firms that fought Trump’s executive orders have forced him to retreat.
articles.snippet: The four law firms that fought Trump’s executive orders have forced him to retreat.

articles.title: Justice Gorsuch’s Tariffs Warning
articles.abstract: Granting a president new power is easy, he said. But taking it back is almost impossible.
articles.snippet: Granting a president new power is easy, he said. But taking it back is almost impossible.

articles.title: Divide Among Supreme Court’s Conservatives Could Test Trump’s Agenda
articles.abstract: In rejecting President Trump’s tariffs, the court’s six conservative justices displayed subtle differences in their views of executive power.
articles.snippet: In rejecting President Trump’s tariffs, the court’s six conservative justices displayed subtle differences in their views of executive power.
